claims.summary: Sodium butyrate decreased LPS-induced depression-like behaviors in mice.
High-fiber inulin diet increased cecal butyrate in aged mice.
High-fiber inulin diet promoted an anti-inflammatory microglial profile in aged mice.
Pharmacologic butyrate treatment decreased expression of IL-1β, TNF, and IL-6 in microglia from aged mice after LPS challenge.
Clostridium butyricum treatment decreased microglial activation in the brain of APP/PS1 mice.

Sodium butyrate exacerbates neuroinflammation in MPTP-induced Parkinson's disease mice model, increasing microglial and astrocyte activation and pro-inflammatory markers such as IL-1β, IL-18, iNOS, and nitric oxide.
Sodium butyrate decreased neuroinflammation in 5XFAD mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced hypothalamic microgliosis in high-fat-diet mice.
Sodium butyrate supplementation decreased neuroinflammatory response in mice with ethanol-induced inflammation, even when gut dysbiosis was worsened by antibiotics.

context.model_system: mouse model of binge-like ethanol drinking

claims.summary: Sodium butyrate decreased neuroinflammation in chronic alcohol-exposed mice.

context.administration_route: oral or systemic (implied by study context)

claims.summary: Sodium butyrate improved motor performance in MPTP-induced Parkinson's disease mice.
Sodium butyrate decreased infarct volume in diabetic stroke mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced colitis severity in DSS-induced ulcerative colitis mice.
Sodium butyrate attenuates microglia-mediated neuroinflammation in cardiac arrest mice.
SCFA supplementation after TBI reduced ventriculomegaly in mice.
Butyrate decreases Aβ42 uptake in endothelial cells under amyloid stress.

contradictions.hypothesis: The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, dose_level, model_system, time_horizon. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_33785315::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, dose_level, microbiome_status, model_system, time_horizon, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_36555338::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, barrier_status, disease_state, dose_level, model_system, time_horizon, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_36709599::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, dose_level, model_system, time_horizon, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_38340407::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: barrier_status, disease_state, dose_level, model_system, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_39962509::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

experiment_proposals.title: Test whether dose_level, administration_route, model_system moderates the effect of sodium butyrate on neuroinflammation
experiment_proposals.rationale: Claims pmid_32556930::C1 and pmid_33785315::C1 may only disagree because context axes differ: dose_level, administration_route, model_system. A factorial design can separate true sign flips from hidden moderators.
design: Run a factorial intervention varying dose_level, administration_route, model_system alongside sodium butyrate vs control.
Measure neuroinflammation and any mechanistic mediators tied to ['astrocyte activation', 'microglial activation', 'pro-inflammatory markers'].
Include enough power to detect interaction effects, not just main effects.
predictions: If claim pmid_32556930::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should increase neuroinflammation in context A.
If claim pmid_33785315::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should decrease neuroinflammation in context B.
If the moderator hypothesis is right, the effect sign should depend on the manipulated context axis rather than stay globally fixed.
